title: PIN-30: Market open/closed indicator
description: Linear PIN-30: prices not updating when market is closed leaves users guessing. Add a Market Open/Closed badge to MenuBarPopoverView header, MainWindowView toolbar, and a colored dot to MenuBarLabel. View-local TimelineView pattern (mirror LastUpdatedLabel), no QuoteEngine changes. Plan: ~/.claude/plans/pin-30-replicated-blossom.md and Linear comment 3b93511a-e17d-4564-a8d2-21f819a2f330.
notes: Implemented: MarketStatusBadge view (full pill-style for popover/toolbar; compact dot for menu bar label). MarketStatusFormatterTests added (TDD red->green). Wired into MenuBarPopoverView header, MainWindowView toolbar (.principal), MenuBarLabel. Hit runner hang with TimelineView in MenuBarExtra label — fixed by skipping TimelineView for .compact style; menu bar dot now refreshes on each engine poll instead of independent timer. Full xcodebuild test suite green (13 suites, including new MarketStatusFormatterTests). Note: SwiftUI views are smoke-tested manually per CLAUDE.md — actual GUI verification still needs user.
close_reason: Implemented MarketStatusBadge across popover, main window toolbar, and menu bar label. All tests green. Committed in 266c60f.

title: Verify watchlist persists across app restarts
description: WatchlistStore writes through a SwiftData ModelContext (context.save() on add/remove/reorder) and StockAlertsApp constructs the container with the default initializer: `ModelContainer(for: WatchedSymbol.self, PriceAlert.self)`, which should persist on-disk to Application Support. However, there is no test verifying the on-disk round-trip — all existing WatchlistStoreTests use TestHelpers.makeInMemoryContainer(). We need to confirm (and guard with a regression test) that a WatchedSymbol inserted during one app launch is still there on the next launch.
design:
Add a WatchlistStorePersistenceTests file under StockAlertsTests/ that:
1. Creates a temp directory URL for the store file (FileManager.default.temporaryDirectory.appendingPathComponent(UUID()...).
2. Builds container A with ModelConfiguration(url: tempURL), WatchlistStore A, adds symbols, saves, releases both.
3. Builds container B with the same URL and asserts WatchlistStore B sees the symbols.
4. Remove the temp file in tearDown.

Remember the ModelContext lifetime gotcha (CLAUDE.md): the test must retain the ModelContainer for the duration of each step — don't just keep the context.

No production code changes are expected unless the test reveals a real bug in StockAlertsApp.init() container construction.
acceptance_criteria:
- Manual smoke test: launch app, add AAPL + MSFT to the watchlist, quit the app (Cmd-Q), relaunch — both tickers reappear in the sidebar in the same order.
- Automated test exercising the real on-disk container at a test-scoped URL (ModelConfiguration(url:)), writing with one container instance and reading with a fresh one. Pattern should mirror the subprocess test idea but may stay in-process if a separate ModelContainer over the same .store URL is enough to simulate relaunch.
- If the test fails, fix root cause (likely a ModelConfiguration(isStoredInMemoryOnly:) or a sandbox-container URL issue); do not simply add a shim.
close_reason: Automated test WatchlistStorePersistenceTests.symbolsPersist_acrossContainerInstances_onDisk added and passing. Verified on-disk round-trip: a WatchedSymbol written via one ModelContainer is readable via a fresh ModelContainer at the same store URL. No production change required — default ModelContainer init already persists to Application Support. Manual Cmd-Q smoke test still pending in the live app.

title: Display last-updated timestamp for Finnhub quote data
description:
The polling loop in QuoteEngine.tick() fetches fresh quotes from Finnhub every pollInterval seconds (default 10s, user-configurable) but the UI gives no indication of when the currently-displayed prices were last pulled. When the market is closed, the app is asleep, the network is down, or the user has been away from their machine, prices go stale silently. Users need a visible 'as of' marker so they can judge freshness at a glance.

QuoteEngine today publishes only `quotes` and `lastError` (StockAlerts/Engine/QuoteEngine.swift:6-7). It does not track a last-success Date.
design:
Core:
1. Add a `now: @Sendable () -> Date` closure to QuoteEngine.init (defaults to `Date.init`) so tests can inject a fixed clock. This mirrors the existing `isMarketOpen` injection seam in QuoteEngine.
2. In tick()'s success branch (currently QuoteEngine.swift:52-54), right after `lastError = nil`, set `lastSuccessfulFetch = now()`. Do not touch it in the error branches.
3. Write tests in QuoteEngineTests.swift using a fixed `{ Date(timeIntervalSince1970: 1_000_000) }` closure:
   - tick_setsLastSuccessfulFetch_onSuccess
   - tick_preservesLastSuccessfulFetch_onError (use the existing ToggleableQuoteService to flip success → failure)
   - tick_lastSuccessfulFetchNil_beforeFirstSuccess

UI:
1. Small SwiftUI helper (e.g. RelativeUpdatedLabel or a View extension) that takes `Date?` and renders '—' / 'Updated Ns ago' / 'Updated at HH:MM'. Use a TimelineView(.periodic(from: .now, by: 5)) so it self-refreshes without wiring a Timer.
2. Place it in the MainWindowView sidebar footer (below the 'Add symbol' HStack) and in MenuBarPopoverView's footer/status row — both are SwiftUI views, so smoke-test them manually per CLAUDE.md.

Out of scope (follow-up beads if wanted):
- Per-symbol freshness (each row's own timestamp).
- Persisting the last-fetch timestamp across app restarts.
acceptance_criteria:
- QuoteEngine exposes a new `@Published private(set) var lastSuccessfulFetch: Date?` that is set on every successful tick() and NEVER cleared on error (a failed attempt must not erase the most recent known-good timestamp).
- The main window's sidebar (MainWindowView) shows a subtle, small-font footer like 'Updated 12s ago' — relative when < 1h, else absolute (e.g. '2:14 PM'). It auto-ticks at least every 5s so the relative label stays current without refetching.
- The MenuBarPopoverView shows the same marker in its footer/status row.
- Before the first successful fetch, the label reads '—' (or is hidden). Do not show 'Updated 0s ago' on a stale-never state.
- If `lastError != nil` and `lastSuccessfulFetch` is older than ~60s, the marker surfaces mildly (e.g., amber tint) so the user can see the data is degrading. This is a nice-to-have — nail the happy path first.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #4 (squash-merged as e66feed on main). QuoteEngine exposes @Published lastSuccessfulFetch: Date?, set on every successful tick() and never cleared on error; new injectable now closure (mirroring isMarketOpen) keeps the timestamp deterministic in tests. UI: LastUpdatedLabel renders 'Updated Ns ago' / 'Nm ago' / 'at HH:MM' (with TimelineView.periodic self-refresh every 5s) in MainWindowView's sidebar footer and MenuBarPopoverView's footer. Pure formatter has 7 unit tests; engine has 5 new tests; full suite 119/119 green. Out of scope (potential follow-ups): per-symbol freshness, persistence across restarts, amber tint when stale + lastError.

title: Open Apple Stocks.app for a watched ticker
description:
Currently a user can see quotes in StockAlerts but cannot jump to Apple's built-in Stocks.app for a richer chart and news feed. Add an affordance that launches Stocks.app, ideally pre-scoped to the selected ticker.

Plausible entry points (pick one — don't build all three):
1. Context-menu item 'Open in Stocks' on each sidebar row in MainWindowView.
2. 'Open in Stocks' button in SymbolDetailView's header toolbar.
3. 'Open in Stocks' on each row in the MenuBarPopoverView list.
design:
Apple's Stocks.app on macOS handles the 'stocks://' URL scheme and accepts symbol deep-links (e.g., 'stocks://?symbol=AAPL' — verify current behaviour on the target macOS before shipping; Apple has changed this over the years).

Sketch:
1. Thin helper StocksAppLauncher with a single @MainActor static func open(symbol: String) that builds the URL and calls NSWorkspace.shared.open. Inject a seam (protocol / closure) for tests so we never actually launch Stocks.app in CI.
2. Pick the context-menu entry point (option 1 above) to start — minimal UI surface, easy to reach.
3. Add a SwiftUI-level smoke test (follow the 'only exception: SwiftUI views' rule in CLAUDE.md — manual test in the running app, document the click path in the PR).
acceptance_criteria:
- Invoking the action for ticker AAPL launches Stocks.app (bringing it frontmost) and, if the Stocks.app URL scheme supports it, lands on AAPL's symbol page.
- If Stocks.app is not installed (e.g., an older macOS or a stripped image), the action is either hidden or surfaces a graceful non-fatal error — do not crash.
- Action stays on the main actor; uses NSWorkspace.open(_:configuration:completionHandler:) rather than blocking calls.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #2 (squash-merged as 4b309f2 on main). Final form: 'Open in Stocks' button as a primary-action toolbar item on SymbolDetailView, built on StocksAppLauncher (NSWorkspace.open behind a URLOpening protocol seam, 4 unit tests). Manual smoke test still pending; the button opens stocks://?symbol=<SYM> via NSWorkspace, which is graceful/non-fatal if Stocks.app isn't installed.

value: SwiftUI TimelineView inside a MenuBarExtra label freezes the SwiftUI runtime: xcodebuild test fails with 'The test runner hung before establishing connection' even though the build succeeds. Use TimelineView freely inside popover/window content, but in MenuBarExtra { ... } label: { Foo() } the Foo view (and any nested view it renders) must be static — recompute state by deriving from observed @Published / @AppStorage instead. Discovered planning PIN-30 market open/closed badge.
